title: eriochilus collinus subsp. collinus
intro: Eriochilus collinus subsp. collinus, also known as the Western Greenhood, is a rare species of orchid found in southwestern Western Australia. It has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and acting as an antispasmodic. Traditionally, the herbal preparation of Eriochilus collinus subsp. collinus has been made by boiling the roots in water or using it as a tea to extract its medicinal properties.
